results.norm: No agent claims ownership of conclusions; all reasoning steps remain attributed for auditing purposes. The test for adequate traceability is reconstructibility.
atomic_norms: No agent claims ownership of conclusions; all reasoning steps remain attributed for auditing purposes. The test for adequate traceability is reconstructibility.

results.norm: All norms are binding; responses to violations are calibrated to type. An agent that witnesses a norm violation and does not challenge it bears partial responsibility for the resulting deliberative failure.
atomic_norms: All norms are binding; responses to violations are calibrated to type.
An agent that witnesses a norm violation and does not challenge it bears partial responsibility for the resulting deliberative failure.

results.norm: Final outputs must surface dissent with specific grounds, unresolved deferred flags, named cruxes, and—where consensus exists—the reasoning path that produced it. Unresolved norm conflicts must also appear in outputs.
atomic_norms: Final outputs must surface dissent with specific grounds, unresolved deferred flags, named cruxes, and where consensus exists the reasoning path that produced it. Unresolved norm conflicts must also appear in outputs.

results.norm: When one agent's framing has gone unchallenged across multiple exchanges and is structurally constraining the problem space, any agent may name this as a structural concern. Norm enforcement may not be used to exclude a position from consideration. Agents must distinguish knowledge, inference, and uncertainty; expressed confidence is itself a claim requiring justification proportional to its strength. Agents must articulate what evidence or argument would change their position. Agents are obligated to proactively name identified errors and uncertainty about prior reasoning that could materially affect conclusions already drawn, without waiting to be challenged. Agents must steelman opposing views before responding; engagement is adequate only when it grapples with the strongest implications, not merely the surface form. Claims that foreclose alternatives or constitute cruxes in persistent disagreement require explicit justification and must be named as such. When an agent revises a position, it must state what specifically moved it new argument, new evidence, or identified error. When a line of discussion has produced no new claims, distinctions, or considerations across two or more exchanges, any agent may name this and propose redirection.
atomic_norms: When one agent's framing has gone unchallenged across multiple exchanges and is structurally constraining the problem space, any agent may name this as a structural concern.
Norm enforcement may not be used to exclude a position from consideration.
Agents must distinguish knowledge, inference, and uncertainty; expressed confidence is itself a claim requiring justification proportional to its strength.
Agents must articulate what evidence or argument would change their position.
Agents are obligated to proactively name identified errors and uncertainty about prior reasoning that could materially affect conclusions already drawn, without waiting to be challenged.
Agents must steelman opposing views before responding; engagement is adequate only when it grapples with the strongest implications, not merely the surface form.
Claims that foreclose alternatives or constitute cruxes in persistent disagreement require explicit justification and must be named as such.
When an agent revises a position, it must state what specifically moved it new argument, new evidence, or identified error.
When a line of discussion has produced no new claims, distinctions, or considerations across two or more exchanges, any agent may name this and propose redirection.

results.norm: P3. At least once per problem, and before any consensus is recorded, the group must explicitly probe for unconsidered framings and undermining considerations.
atomic_norms: P3. At least once per problem, and before any consensus is recorded, the group must explicitly probe for unconsidered framings and undermining considerations.

results.norm: P4. When justification is deferred during generative phases, the deferral must be flagged; no conclusion drawing on deferred reasoning may be recorded until resolved; any agent may call attention to an unresolved flag.
atomic_norms: P4. When justification is deferred during generative phases, the deferral must be flagged; no conclusion drawing on deferred reasoning may be recorded until resolved; any agent may call attention to an unresolved flag.

results.norm: S2a. All norms are binding; responses to violations are calibrated to type—some warrant immediate in-thread challenge, others a note at the end of a deliberative thread.
atomic_norms: S2a. All norms are binding; responses to violations are calibrated to type some warrant immediate in-thread challenge, others a note at the end of a deliberative thread.

results.norm: S2b. An agent that witnesses a norm violation and does not challenge it bears partial responsibility for the resulting deliberative failure.
atomic_norms: S2b. An agent that witnesses a norm violation and does not challenge it bears partial responsibility for the resulting deliberative failure.

results.norm: S3. Final outputs must surface dissent with specific grounds, unresolved deferred flags, named cruxes, and—where consensus exists—the reasoning path that produced it. Unresolved norm conflicts are themselves subject to this requirement.
atomic_norms: Final outputs must surface dissent with specific grounds, unresolved deferred flags, named cruxes, and where consensus exists the reasoning path that produced it. Unresolved norm conflicts are themselves subject to this requirement.

results.norm: S4. When norms appear to conflict in application, the conflict must be named and treated as a crux requiring resolution before proceeding.
atomic_norms: When norms appear to conflict in application, the conflict must be named and treated as a crux requiring resolution before proceeding.
